Defendants' Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Apr-21-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION.

(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry.)

The agreement is not substantively unconscionable because it is not overly harsh and so one-sided that it shocks the conscience. There is no requirement that an arbitration agreement explicitly state the parties waive their right to trial by jury. The primary case on which Ms. Delos Reyes relies, Titan Group v. Sonoma Valley County Sanitation District (1985) 164 CalApp.3d 1122, 1127-28, is inapposite. In Titan, there was no contractual provision mandating arbitration but rather a statement that the parties will arbitrate if they "mutually agree to do so." (Ibid.) This difference is significant because it provided some doubt as to whether the parties were mandated to arbitrate and therefore forego their jury trial right. No such ambiguity exists here because the arbitration agreement states the parties "shall submit to mandatory and exclusive binding arbitration." (Ray Declaration, Exh. A, p. 2.) As to the issue of discovery, there is nothing in the arbitration agreement indicating the parties would be afforded different amounts or methods of discovery. It appears both Ms. Delos Reyes and Marin Software would both be subject to the AAA rules regarding discovery.

Because the only substantively unconscionable provision-which Defendants concede is substantively unconscionable-is the requirement that the arbitrator shift fees and costs to the prevailing party, that provision will be severed. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed form of order and approved as to form, to be submitted in Dept. 206. Judge: Teri L. Jackson; Clerk: R. Veluz; Court Reporter: Dana DiBasilio Tognini, CSR#10118 (415)793-8811. (206)

Defendant Marin Software, Inc., A Delaware Corporation, Cristina Sava Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Apr-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Vanessa Deniston of DOLAN LAW FIRM (510-893-2700), appearing for plaintiff.
Walter M. Stella of MILLER LAW GROUP (415-464-4300), appearing for defendants Marin Software Incorporated and Cristina Sava.

DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION. (Tentative ruling was given to counsel prior to hearing.)

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Marin Software Incorporated and Cristina Sava's motion to compel arbitration is granted and the entire action is stayed pending completion of arbitration.
Per Vianna v. Doctors' Management Co. (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1186, 1190, the arbitration clause covers all claims alleged in the complaint because the claims are rooted in the general employment relationship created by the offer letter. This includes the sole cause of action alleged against Ms. Sava for harassment in violation of the FEHA. The harassment claim is "intimately founded in and intertwined" with the underlying contractual obligations contemplated by the offer letter, which effectively created the employment relationship between Plaintiff Nancy Delos Reyes and Marin Software. (See Boucher v. Alliance Title Co., Inc. (2005) 127 CalApp.4th 262, 271-72.) Further, the unilateral modification clause does not necessarily render the offer letter unconscionable, as "[t]he implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing limits the employer's authority to unilaterally modify the arbitration agreement and saves that agreement from being illusory and thus unconscionable." (Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc. (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 695, 708.)

Ms. Delos Reyes has not shown the arbitration agreement is sufficiently procedurally and substantively unconscionable to render it unenforceable. The court finds the agreement is only slightly procedurally unconscionable and not substantively unconscionable once the fee-shifting provision is severed.
As to procedural unconscionability, Defendants concede the agreement is a contract of adhesion and that they did not attach the applicable AAA rules. However, per McManus v. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 76, 91, and Peng v. First Republic Bank (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472, these two qualities of the agreement by themselves are insufficient to render the agreement unenforceable. Although Ms. Delos Reyes declares she was unaware of the arbitration agreement, there is evidence showing she signed the offer letter and initialed each page via DocuSign. (Delos Reyes Declaration, 8-10; Ray Declaration, Exh. A.) The offer letter states, "Your signature will acknowledge that you have read and understood and agreed to the terms and conditions of this offer letter'." (Ray Declaration, Exh. A, p. 3.) Further, the arbitration clause, contained in a short three-page letter, is set apart in a separately numbered paragraph with an underlined and boldfaced heading reading "Arbitration." (Ray Declaration, Exh. A, p. 2.) Although Marin Software presented the arbitration agreement on a take-it-or-leave-it basis, the above facts indicate the arbitration agreement only has a moderate degree of procedural unconscionability.
(Mini-minutes continued, see next entry.)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Apr-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS MARIN SOFTWARE, INC. and CRISTINA SAVA's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is continued to April 20, 2017, to be heard in Department 206, at 9:30 a.m., by Presiding Judge Teri L. Jackson. No court reporter will be provided. If a party desires a court reporter, that party should confer with all other parties and make arrangements for a single reporter. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =(302/HK)
